鬠ｿCHAPTER ONE: BACKGROUND INFORMATION

1¥.1 INTRODUCTION

Every笏ｬﾃ｡morning, millions笏ｬﾃ｡of people笏ｬﾃ｡around笏ｬﾃ｡the world stand笏ｬﾃ｡before笏ｬﾃ｡their wardrobes and face笏ｬﾃ｡the same silent笏ｬﾃ｡question:笏ｬﾃ｡*What笏ｬﾃ｡should I wear today?*笏ｬﾃ｡What笏ｬﾃ｡seems笏ｬﾃ｡like a trivial decision carries笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡hidden笏ｬﾃ｡cost¥. Research笏ｬﾃ｡in笏ｬﾃ｡behavioral psychology笏ｬﾃ｡has shown that the average person spends 10 to笏ｬﾃ｡15 minutes daily笏ｬﾃ｡deciding what to wear, amounting to over笏ｬﾃ｡90 hours笏ｬﾃ｡per year lost笏ｬﾃ｡to a笏ｬﾃ｡single recurring笏ｬﾃ｡choice笏ｬﾃ｡¥(Baumeister笏ｬﾃ｡& Tierney, 2011¥)¥. At笏ｬﾃ｡the same time, studies笏ｬﾃ｡by笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡Waste笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡Resources笏ｬﾃ｡Action Programme ¥(WRAP, 2017¥) reveal笏ｬﾃ｡that most笏ｬﾃ｡individuals笏ｬﾃ｡regularly笏ｬﾃ｡wear笏ｬﾃ｡only笏ｬﾃ｡20 to 30 percent of the笏ｬﾃ｡clothing笏ｬﾃ｡they own笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ the笏ｬﾃ｡rest sits idle, forgotten in closets and drawers¥. 

This pattern笏ｬﾃ｡of underutilization is not笏ｬﾃ｡just笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡personal inconvenience; it is笏ｬﾃ｡an笏ｬﾃ｡environmental笏ｬﾃ｡crisis¥. The global笏ｬﾃ｡fashion笏ｬﾃ｡industry produces笏ｬﾃ｡an笏ｬﾃ｡estimated 92 million tonnes笏ｬﾃ｡of textile waste annually, making笏ｬﾃ｡it the笏ｬﾃ｡second most polluting industry in the world after笏ｬﾃ｡petroleum笏ｬﾃ｡¥(United笏ｬﾃ｡Nations Environment笏ｬﾃ｡Programme, 2019¥)¥. Much笏ｬﾃ｡of this waste stems笏ｬﾃ｡not笏ｬﾃ｡from worn¥-out garments but from clothing笏ｬﾃ｡that was笏ｬﾃ｡purchased笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡never笏ｬﾃ｡fully笏ｬﾃ｡used¥. In笏ｬﾃ｡Kenya笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡across笏ｬﾃ｡East Africa, the growing笏ｬﾃ｡influx of affordable笏ｬﾃ｡fast笏ｬﾃ｡fashion and笏ｬﾃ｡imported笏ｬﾃ｡secondhand clothing笏ｬﾃ｡¥(commonly笏ｬﾃ｡known as笏ｬﾃ｡*mitumba*¥) has expanded笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobes rapidly, yet笏ｬﾃ｡without笏ｬﾃ｡any笏ｬﾃ｡tools笏ｬﾃ｡to笏ｬﾃ｡help individuals笏ｬﾃ｡manage, organize, or make full笏ｬﾃ｡use of what笏ｬﾃ｡they already own¥.

The root笏ｬﾃ｡cause is笏ｬﾃ｡a lack笏ｬﾃ｡of visibility¥. Without笏ｬﾃ｡a digital笏ｬﾃ｡record笏ｬﾃ｡of their笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe, people笏ｬﾃ｡cannot easily笏ｬﾃ｡see笏ｬﾃ｡what they have, plan笏ｬﾃ｡outfits efficiently, or identify笏ｬﾃ｡items they笏ｬﾃ｡have笏ｬﾃ｡stopped笏ｬﾃ｡wearing¥. Existing笏ｬﾃ｡solutions such笏ｬﾃ｡as manual笏ｬﾃ｡spreadsheets or笏ｬﾃ｡basic笏ｬﾃ｡closet apps笏ｬﾃ｡require笏ｬﾃ｡tedious data笏ｬﾃ｡entry, making笏ｬﾃ｡them笏ｬﾃ｡impractical for everyday笏ｬﾃ｡use and笏ｬﾃ｡leading笏ｬﾃ｡to low笏ｬﾃ｡adoption rates¥.

Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit addresses this gap¥. It笏ｬﾃ｡is an AI¥-powered mobile application that笏ｬﾃ｡uses笏ｬﾃ｡computer笏ｬﾃ｡vision to笏ｬﾃ｡digitize a笏ｬﾃ｡user's wardrobe by笏ｬﾃ｡simply笏ｬﾃ｡photographing each笏ｬﾃ｡clothing笏ｬﾃ｡item¥. The system笏ｬﾃ｡automatically笏ｬﾃ｡identifies笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡garment's笏ｬﾃ｡category and笏ｬﾃ｡dominant笏ｬﾃ｡color, stores笏ｬﾃ｡it笏ｬﾃ｡in a digital笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe, and uses笏ｬﾃ｡an笏ｬﾃ｡AI笏ｬﾃ｡Stylist to recommend笏ｬﾃ｡complete笏ｬﾃ｡outfits from笏ｬﾃ｡the user's existing collection¥. The笏ｬﾃ｡aim笏ｬﾃ｡is threefold: to eliminate笏ｬﾃ｡the time笏ｬﾃ｡wasted on manual wardrobe management, to reduce笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡daily笏ｬﾃ｡cognitive笏ｬﾃ｡burden笏ｬﾃ｡of outfit笏ｬﾃ｡selection, and to increase the proportion笏ｬﾃ｡of owned笏ｬﾃ｡clothing that is actively笏ｬﾃ｡worn笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ thereby笏ｬﾃ｡promoting笏ｬﾃ｡more笏ｬﾃ｡sustainable consumption habits¥.

This笏ｬﾃ｡project investigates the effectiveness笏ｬﾃ｡of Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit through笏ｬﾃ｡four笏ｬﾃ｡controlled笏ｬﾃ｡experiments measuring笏ｬﾃ｡digitization笏ｬﾃ｡speed, AI笏ｬﾃ｡classification笏ｬﾃ｡accuracy, outfit笏ｬﾃ｡decision笏ｬﾃ｡time, and wardrobe utilization, with all笏ｬﾃ｡results evaluated笏ｬﾃ｡for笏ｬﾃ｡statistical笏ｬﾃ｡significance using笏ｬﾃ｡paired笏ｬﾃ｡t¥-tests at笏ｬﾃ｡the 95% confidence level¥.

1¥.5 STATEMENT OF PROBLEM 

Globally, the fashion industry produces over 92 million tonnes of textile waste annually, making it the second most polluting industry in the world¥. A significant driver of this waste is consumer behavior ﾎ禿僌ｶ people buy more clothes than they need and fail to wear much of what they own¥. Research shows that the average person uses only 20 to 30 percent of their wardrobe regularly, while the rest remains idle¥.

In Kenya and across East Africa, the growing availability of affordable fast fashion and imported secondhand clothing ¥(*mitumba*¥) means wardrobes are expanding, but utilization is not keeping pace¥. At the individual level, managing a wardrobe remains an entirely manual and impractical process¥. There is no efficient way to catalogue what one owns, no system to help combine items into outfits, and no mechanism to identify underused garments¥. This leads to four compounding problems:

- __Time waste笏ｬﾃ｡__ﾎ禿僌ｶ manually cataloging or even remembering what you own is impractical for most people¥.
- __Decision fatigue ﾎ禿僌ｶ __choosing an outfit daily from dozens or hundreds of items drains mental energy and slows productivity¥.
- __Clothing under utilization ﾎ禿僌ｶ __without visibility into the full wardrobe, people repeatedly wear the same few items and forget about the rest¥.
- __Unnecessary spending ﾎ禿僌ｶ __users purchase new clothes for occasions when suitable items already exist in their wardrobe¥.

There is currently no widely available, affordable solution that combines automated wardrobe digitization with AI¥-powered outfit recommendations targeted at everyday users¥.

1¥.6 SIGNIFICANCE AND JUSTIFICATION

This project is significant because it applies artificial intelligence to a universal, everyday problem that affects virtually every person who owns clothing¥. By automating wardrobe management and outfit selection, Fashion Fit delivers measurable value in three areas:

First, it笏ｬﾃ｡__saves time__¥. The application reduces a 7¥-minute manual digitization process to under 2 minutes and cuts a 5¥-minute outfit decision down to approximately 1 minute¥. Over the course of a year, these savings compound into dozens of hours returned to the user¥.

Second, it笏ｬﾃ｡__promotes sustainability__¥. By helping users discover and wear underused items in their wardrobe, Fashion Fit reduces the impulse to purchase new clothing unnecessarily¥. The WRAP ¥(2017¥) study found that extending the active life of a garment by just 9 months reduces its carbon, water, and waste footprint by 20 to 30 percent¥. Increasing wardrobe utilization from 36% to 76% ﾎ禿僌ｶ as demonstrated in this project ﾎ禿僌ｶ represents a significant step toward responsible consumption¥.

Third, it笏ｬﾃ｡__democratizes fashion technology__¥. AI¥-powered styling has traditionally been available only to professional stylists or luxury consumers¥. Fashion Fit makes this capability accessible to any smartphone user, including students and young professionals in Kenya and across East Africa¥.

1¥.6¥.1 LINKAGE TO EMERGING ISSUES

- __Sustainable Development Goal 12 ¥(Responsible Consumption and Production¥)__: Fashion Fit directly promotes the principle of using what you already own before buying more, reducing textile waste at the consumer level¥.
- __Artificial Intelligence in everyday life: 笏ｬﾃ｡__The project demonstrates a practical, beneficial application of AI beyond industrial or corporate settings, showing how machine learning can solve personal, daily problems¥.
- __Digital transformation in Kenya: __笏ｬﾃ｡With smartphone penetration in Kenya exceeding 60%, mobile¥-first solutions like Fashion Fit align with the country's digital economy agenda under Kenya Vision 2030¥.
- __Fast fashion and textile waste: __As global awareness of the fashion industry's environmental footprint grows, tools that extend the useful life of existing garments address a pressing and timely concern¥.
- __Youth innovation and entrepreneurship:__ The project demonstrates that young Kenyans can create technology¥-driven solutions to real¥-world problems, contributing to the country's growing reputation as a hub for African tech innovation¥.

1¥.7 MERITS 

- __Instant笏ｬﾃ｡Wardrobe Digitization: 笏ｬﾃ｡__A笏ｬﾃ｡single photograph笏ｬﾃ｡is笏ｬﾃ｡all it takes to add笏ｬﾃ｡a clothing笏ｬﾃ｡item to the笏ｬﾃ｡digital笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe ﾎ禿僌ｶ the AI identifies the category and笏ｬﾃ｡color in seconds, turning a笏ｬﾃ｡tedious manual笏ｬﾃ｡process into a one¥-tap笏ｬﾃ｡action¥.
- __AI¥-Powered Personal笏ｬﾃ｡Stylist:__笏ｬﾃ｡The app笏ｬﾃ｡acts笏ｬﾃ｡as an always¥-available personal stylist, generating complete笏ｬﾃ｡outfit combinations笏ｬﾃ｡from笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡user's own笏ｬﾃ｡clothes笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ a笏ｬﾃ｡service笏ｬﾃ｡that traditionally笏ｬﾃ｡costs笏ｬﾃ｡thousands笏ｬﾃ｡of shillings when笏ｬﾃ｡done by笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡professional¥.

- __Reduces Daily笏ｬﾃ｡Decision Fatigue: 笏ｬﾃ｡__Instead笏ｬﾃ｡of staring at a笏ｬﾃ｡full笏ｬﾃ｡closet every笏ｬﾃ｡morning trying笏ｬﾃ｡to decide, the笏ｬﾃ｡user笏ｬﾃ｡opens笏ｬﾃ｡the app and receives ready¥-made笏ｬﾃ｡outfit options instantly ﾎ禿僌ｶ saving笏ｬﾃ｡mental energy for more笏ｬﾃ｡important decisions throughout笏ｬﾃ｡the day¥.
- __Runs on Any Smartphone: __笏ｬﾃ｡The app requires no special hardware, no expensive笏ｬﾃ｡gadgets, and no complicated setup笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ just a phone with a camera and笏ｬﾃ｡an笏ｬﾃ｡internet connection, making it accessible to anyone笏ｬﾃ｡from a secondary school student to a working笏ｬﾃ｡professional¥.
- __Fights笏ｬﾃ｡Textile Waste at笏ｬﾃ｡the Source: __By helping笏ｬﾃ｡users maximize笏ｬﾃ｡what笏ｬﾃ｡they already own, Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit tackles笏ｬﾃ｡the root cause of clothing笏ｬﾃ｡waste笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ not笏ｬﾃ｡at笏ｬﾃ｡the factory笏ｬﾃ｡or笏ｬﾃ｡the landfill, but at the individual's笏ｬﾃ｡closet, where purchasing笏ｬﾃ｡decisions笏ｬﾃ｡are made¥.

1¥.8 DEMERITS 

- __Internet Dependency:__ 笏ｬﾃ｡The笏ｬﾃ｡AI笏ｬﾃ｡image笏ｬﾃ｡processing笏ｬﾃ｡currently笏ｬﾃ｡relies笏ｬﾃ｡on cloud¥-based models, which require笏ｬﾃ｡an active internet connection¥. In areas with limited connectivity, this笏ｬﾃ｡may笏ｬﾃ｡slow笏ｬﾃ｡down the笏ｬﾃ｡digitization process¥. Future笏ｬﾃ｡versions笏ｬﾃ｡could implement on¥-device processing to笏ｬﾃ｡work笏ｬﾃ｡offline¥.
- __Limited笏ｬﾃ｡Category笏ｬﾃ｡Range:笏ｬﾃ｡笏ｬﾃ｡__The current笏ｬﾃ｡version笏ｬﾃ｡recognizes six笏ｬﾃ｡clothing categories ¥(top, bottom, dress, shoes, outerwear, accessory¥)¥. More笏ｬﾃ｡specialized笏ｬﾃ｡items, such笏ｬﾃ｡as traditional笏ｬﾃ｡garments, sportswear, or formal笏ｬﾃ｡subcategories, are笏ｬﾃ｡not yet distinguished, presenting笏ｬﾃ｡an笏ｬﾃ｡opportunity for expansion¥.
- __Smartphone Requirement: __The笏ｬﾃ｡application is笏ｬﾃ｡currently笏ｬﾃ｡designed笏ｬﾃ｡for smartphones, which means笏ｬﾃ｡users笏ｬﾃ｡without access笏ｬﾃ｡to a smartphone would笏ｬﾃ｡need笏ｬﾃ｡one笏ｬﾃ｡to use Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit¥. However, with笏ｬﾃ｡Kenya's smartphone笏ｬﾃ｡adoption笏ｬﾃ｡rate growing笏ｬﾃ｡rapidly笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡device笏ｬﾃ｡costs笏ｬﾃ｡decreasing year笏ｬﾃ｡on笏ｬﾃ｡year, this barrier笏ｬﾃ｡is expected笏ｬﾃ｡to diminish over time¥.

     

1¥.9 ASSUMPTIONS AND PRECAUTIONS

1¥.9¥.1 ASSUMPTIONS

- __Smartphone Availability:__ Participants will have access to a smartphone with a functioning camera capable of running the Fashion Fit application during all testing sessions¥.
- __Representative Wardrobe Items:__笏ｬﾃ｡笏ｬﾃ｡The clothing items used in testing are representative of typical Kenyan wardrobes, including a reasonable mix of categories such as tops, bottoms, dresses, shoes, outerwear, and accessories¥.
- __Participant Honesty: 笏ｬﾃ｡__Participants will perform all tasks genuinely and honestly, without rushing or deliberately slowing down during either the manual or app¥-based methods¥.
- __Photograph Quality: 笏ｬﾃ｡__Photographs taken by participants will be of reasonable quality, with adequate lighting and the clothing item clearly visible, suitable for AI analysis without major distortions affecting the classification¥.
- __Ground Truth Reliability: __The two independent human labelers used to establish ground truth labels in Test 2 are competent, unbiased, and consistent in their categorization of clothing items¥.

1¥.9¥.2 PRECAUTIONS

- __Order Alternation: 笏ｬﾃ｡__The order of testing ¥(manual method first vs Fashion Fit first¥) was alternated across participants to control for learning effects and ensure neither method had an unfair advantage¥.
- __Consistent Timing: __All task duration's were measured using the same stopwatch method with clearly defined start and stop points to eliminate timing inconsistencies across participants¥.
- __Controlled Environment:__笏ｬﾃ｡All testing was conducted in a well¥-lit indoor room under similar lighting conditions to minimize variation in the AI's image recognition performance¥.
- __Independent Labeling__: Ground truth labels for AI accuracy testing were established by two human labels working independently, reducing the risk of individual bias influencing the benchmark data¥.
- __Data Privacy: __All wardrobe photographs and participant data were handled confidentially and used solely for the purposes of this research¥. No personal images were shared publicly¥.
- __Participant Briefing: __Each participant was briefed on the full testing procedure before beginning, ensuring they understood both methods and could perform the tasks without confusion¥.

3¥. __Virtual笏ｬﾃ｡Closet Assistant: An笏ｬﾃ｡AI¥-Driven Approach for笏ｬﾃ｡Intelligent Wardrobe Management and笏ｬﾃ｡Personalized Outfit Recommendation笏ｬﾃ｡¥(IJIRSET, 2025¥)__笏ｬﾃ｡This笏ｬﾃ｡study笏ｬﾃ｡presented an笏ｬﾃ｡AI¥-powered virtual closet assistant that笏ｬﾃ｡automated笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe cataloging using computer笏ｬﾃ｡vision to笏ｬﾃ｡identify clothing by type, color, season, and occasion¥. It笏ｬﾃ｡also笏ｬﾃ｡analyzed usage patterns to highlight underutilized items and promote笏ｬﾃ｡sustainable fashion choices¥. While笏ｬﾃ｡the research笏ｬﾃ｡closely笏ｬﾃ｡aligns with the goals笏ｬﾃ｡of Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit, it笏ｬﾃ｡was conducted笏ｬﾃ｡outside笏ｬﾃ｡Africa笏ｬﾃ｡and did not consider笏ｬﾃ｡the unique笏ｬﾃ｡context笏ｬﾃ｡of the笏ｬﾃ｡Kenyan market笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ including笏ｬﾃ｡the prevalence of secondhand clothing ¥(*mitumba*¥), mobile¥-first internet usage, and the specific笏ｬﾃ｡sustainability笏ｬﾃ｡challenges faced by East笏ｬﾃ｡African consumers¥. The current project adapts these笏ｬﾃ｡concepts笏ｬﾃ｡for the Kenyan context¥.

4¥. __笏ｬﾃ｡Afronomy Chain笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ Overall笏ｬﾃ｡Winner, Young笏ｬﾃ｡Scientists Kenya笏ｬﾃ｡2025笏ｬﾃ｡¥(Kon笏ｬﾃ｡Lual Ajok笏ｬﾃ｡& Ian Mwadiloh, Nova Pioneer Boys High School笏ｬﾃ｡Eldoret¥)__笏ｬﾃ｡The笏ｬﾃ｡2025 YSK overall笏ｬﾃ｡winner was笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡blockchain¥-powered web system that enabled笏ｬﾃ｡real¥-time tracking of public funds to笏ｬﾃ｡curb corruption¥. While笏ｬﾃ｡this笏ｬﾃ｡project is笏ｬﾃ｡in笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡different domain笏ｬﾃ｡¥(governance笏ｬﾃ｡technology¥), it demonstrates the standard笏ｬﾃ｡of笏ｬﾃ｡innovation笏ｬﾃ｡expected笏ｬﾃ｡at the笏ｬﾃ｡YSK competition笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ applying笏ｬﾃ｡advanced笏ｬﾃ｡technology笏ｬﾃ｡¥(blockchain¥) to solve a笏ｬﾃ｡real笏ｬﾃ｡Kenyan problem with笏ｬﾃ｡measurable impact¥. Fashion Fit follows the same approach by applying AI and笏ｬﾃ｡computer笏ｬﾃ｡vision to solve笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡real, everyday笏ｬﾃ｡problem笏ｬﾃ｡of wardrobe management and笏ｬﾃ｡clothing笏ｬﾃ｡waste¥. No笏ｬﾃ｡previous笏ｬﾃ｡YSK entry笏ｬﾃ｡has笏ｬﾃ｡addressed笏ｬﾃ｡AI¥-powered笏ｬﾃ｡fashion technology or笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe sustainability¥.

2¥.2 RELATED RESEARCH

In笏ｬﾃ｡recent笏ｬﾃ｡years, significant笏ｬﾃ｡progress has been made in the笏ｬﾃ｡application笏ｬﾃ｡of artificial intelligence and笏ｬﾃ｡computer笏ｬﾃ｡vision to fashion and笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe management¥. This笏ｬﾃ｡section笏ｬﾃ｡reviews笏ｬﾃ｡key笏ｬﾃ｡research findings笏ｬﾃ｡that are笏ｬﾃ｡directly relevant to the笏ｬﾃ｡development笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡evaluation of Fashion Fit¥.

Research笏ｬﾃ｡in笏ｬﾃ｡cognitive笏ｬﾃ｡psychology has established that repeated decision¥-making笏ｬﾃ｡progressively depletes mental resources, a笏ｬﾃ｡phenomenon known as decision fatigue¥. Baumeister and笏ｬﾃ｡Tierney ¥(2011¥) demonstrated笏ｬﾃ｡that individuals笏ｬﾃ｡who笏ｬﾃ｡face笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡high笏ｬﾃ｡volume笏ｬﾃ｡of daily笏ｬﾃ｡choices笏ｬﾃ｡experience slower笏ｬﾃ｡decision times笏ｬﾃ｡and reduced笏ｬﾃ｡satisfaction笏ｬﾃ｡with their selections¥. When笏ｬﾃ｡applied to fashion, studies show that the笏ｬﾃ｡brain experiences a "switching cost" with笏ｬﾃ｡each micro¥-choice made while browsing笏ｬﾃ｡a wardrobe, particularly when closets contain redundant or conflicting style笏ｬﾃ｡options¥. A 2024笏ｬﾃ｡Accenture report found that 76% of consumers report feeling overwhelmed by笏ｬﾃ｡choice, while 88% have abandoned笏ｬﾃ｡decisions entirely due to decision stress¥. This笏ｬﾃ｡directly supports Fashion Fit's approach of笏ｬﾃ｡narrowing wardrobe options to a笏ｬﾃ｡curated few through AI¥-generated suggestions, reducing the cognitive load users face each笏ｬﾃ｡morning¥.

Global笏ｬﾃ｡research on clothing consumption reveals a severe underutilization crisis¥. According to data笏ｬﾃ｡compiled笏ｬﾃ｡by WRAP ¥(2024¥) and RawShot AI ¥(2026¥), the average garment is worn笏ｬﾃ｡only 7 times before being discarded ﾎ禿僌ｶ a 36% decrease in笏ｬﾃ｡garment utilization compared to 15 years ago¥. Furthermore, 82% of items in a typical wardrobe are worn fewer than three times per笏ｬﾃ｡year, and 40% of consumers笏ｬﾃ｡admit to buying clothes they never wear at笏ｬﾃ｡all¥. Approximately 85% of笏ｬﾃ｡all discarded textiles end up in landfills or are incinerated, despite 95% of them being笏ｬﾃ｡reusable or recyclable¥. These笏ｬﾃ｡statistics underscore the urgency of Fashion Fit's sustainability objective: increasing the笏ｬﾃ｡proportion of a wardrobe that is笏ｬﾃ｡actively worn, thereby reducing unnecessary purchases and笏ｬﾃ｡textile waste at the consumer level¥.

The笏ｬﾃ｡technical feasibility of AI¥-based clothing笏ｬﾃ｡recognition has been well established in academic research¥. Liu et al¥. ¥(2016¥) introduced the DeepFashion笏ｬﾃ｡dataset and demonstrated that convolutional neural笏ｬﾃ｡networks ¥(CNNs¥) could classify笏ｬﾃ｡clothing categories from images with high accuracy¥. More recently, fine¥-grained classification笏ｬﾃ｡frameworks on the FashionNet dataset笏ｬﾃ｡achieved a top¥-1 accuracy of笏ｬﾃ｡89¥.19% on real¥-world e¥-commerce images across 162 subcategories¥. A笏ｬﾃ｡2024 literature review comparing CNNs笏ｬﾃ｡and Vision Transformers found笏ｬﾃ｡that combining both architectures ﾎ禿僌ｶ leveraging笏ｬﾃ｡CNNs for recognizing local patterns笏ｬﾃ｡and Vision笏ｬﾃ｡Transformers for grasping overall笏ｬﾃ｡context ﾎ禿僌ｶ offers the most promising strategy for笏ｬﾃ｡fashion image classification¥. Fashion Fit builds笏ｬﾃ｡upon these proven techniques, applying them within笏ｬﾃ｡a practical smartphone application for personal wardrobe management¥.

Kenya's笏ｬﾃ｡mobile landscape provides a strong foundation for a笏ｬﾃ｡smartphone¥-based solution like Fashion Fit¥. According to the Digital 4笏ｬﾃ｡Africa State of Digital Report ¥(2025¥), 99¥.8% of笏ｬﾃ｡Kenyan internet users aged 16笏ｬﾃ｡to 64 own a smartphone¥. The country has 68¥.8笏ｬﾃ｡million mobile connections against a population of笏ｬﾃ｡57 million, representing a 121% mobile penetration rate, with笏ｬﾃ｡internet users totalling笏ｬﾃ｡27¥.4 million and笏ｬﾃ｡growing over 20% year¥-on¥-year¥. Across East Africa, mobile internet subscriptions reached 138笏ｬﾃ｡million active connections in 2024, driven by affordable smartphones and competitive data pricing¥. This data confirms that a mobile¥-first AI application like Fashion Fit is笏ｬﾃ｡well¥-positioned to reach a large, connected, and growing user base in Kenya笏ｬﾃ｡and across the region without requiring desktop computers笏ｬﾃ｡or specialized infrastructure¥.

Finally, multiple笏ｬﾃ｡recent studies published through IEEE and international research笏ｬﾃ｡journals笏ｬﾃ｡¥(2023ﾎ禿僌ｴ2025¥) have explored intelligent wardrobe management笏ｬﾃ｡systems that use machine learning for automated clothing笏ｬﾃ｡recognition, personalized outfit recommendation, and笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe optimization¥. These systems can笏ｬﾃ｡identify garments by type, color, season, and occasion, and some analyze笏ｬﾃ｡usage patterns to highlight underutilized笏ｬﾃ｡items¥. However, a consistent gap across this body of research is the absence of笏ｬﾃ｡controlled experimental validation with real participants¥. Most笏ｬﾃ｡studies present technical system designs without conducting user¥-based experiments or applying statistical tests to笏ｬﾃ｡measure real¥-world impact¥. Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit addresses this gap directly by conducting four笏ｬﾃ｡controlled experiments with 10 to笏ｬﾃ｡15 participants each, validating all笏ｬﾃ｡findings with paired t¥-tests and笏ｬﾃ｡95% confidence intervals ﾎ禿僌ｶ providing the scientific笏ｬﾃ｡evidence that existing research lacks¥.

2¥.3 SCIENTIFIC CONCEPTS APPLIED

- __Large笏ｬﾃ｡Language Models and笏ｬﾃ｡Multimodal AI笏ｬﾃ｡¥(OpenAI GPT¥-4o笏ｬﾃ｡/ GPT¥-4o¥-mini¥)¥.__笏ｬﾃ｡Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit's笏ｬﾃ｡core笏ｬﾃ｡intelligence笏ｬﾃ｡is笏ｬﾃ｡powered笏ｬﾃ｡by OpenAI's GPT¥-4o and笏ｬﾃ｡GPT¥-4o¥-mini, which are large笏ｬﾃ｡language models笏ｬﾃ｡¥(LLMs¥) built笏ｬﾃ｡on the笏ｬﾃ｡Transformer笏ｬﾃ｡architecture¥. The笏ｬﾃ｡Transformer, introduced by笏ｬﾃ｡Vaswani et al¥.笏ｬﾃ｡¥(2017¥) in笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡paper "Attention Is All You Need," uses笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡mechanism笏ｬﾃ｡called self¥-attention that笏ｬﾃ｡allows the model to weigh the importance笏ｬﾃ｡of different笏ｬﾃ｡parts of an笏ｬﾃ｡input simultaneously笏ｬﾃ｡rather笏ｬﾃ｡than processing笏ｬﾃ｡it笏ｬﾃ｡sequentially¥. GPT¥-4o extends笏ｬﾃ｡this to笏ｬﾃ｡multimodal input, meaning笏ｬﾃ｡it can process both笏ｬﾃ｡text and images in笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡single request¥. When a user photographs笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡clothing笏ｬﾃ｡item, the image笏ｬﾃ｡is sent to GPT¥-4o's笏ｬﾃ｡Vision笏ｬﾃ｡API, which analyzes the visual笏ｬﾃ｡content and returns structured data including笏ｬﾃ｡category, color, pattern, fit, style, and笏ｬﾃ｡brand笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ all in one inference笏ｬﾃ｡call¥.
- __Computer笏ｬﾃ｡Vision Through笏ｬﾃ｡Multimodal AI¥.__笏ｬﾃ｡Rather笏ｬﾃ｡than using笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡traditional笏ｬﾃ｡Convolutional Neural Network ¥(CNN¥) trained笏ｬﾃ｡specifically笏ｬﾃ｡on clothing images, Fashion Fit leverages GPT¥-4o's built¥-in vision capabilities¥. The model笏ｬﾃ｡has笏ｬﾃ｡been pre¥-trained on billions笏ｬﾃ｡of image¥-text pairs, giving笏ｬﾃ｡it broad visual笏ｬﾃ｡understanding that笏ｬﾃ｡includes笏ｬﾃ｡fashion笏ｬﾃ｡items¥. When a clothing笏ｬﾃ｡photograph笏ｬﾃ｡is submitted, the笏ｬﾃ｡model笏ｬﾃ｡interprets the image's content ﾎ禿僌ｶ identifying shapes, colors, textures, and patterns ﾎ禿僌ｶ and maps笏ｬﾃ｡these visual features to clothing categories and attributes¥. This笏ｬﾃ｡approach achieves high accuracy without requiring a custom¥-trained image笏ｬﾃ｡classification model¥.
- __Natural Language Processing笏ｬﾃ｡¥(NLP¥) and Prompt笏ｬﾃ｡Engineering¥.笏ｬﾃ｡__The笏ｬﾃ｡AI笏ｬﾃ｡Stylist and笏ｬﾃ｡Style Coach features笏ｬﾃ｡rely笏ｬﾃ｡on carefully笏ｬﾃ｡engineered prompts sent笏ｬﾃ｡to GPT¥-4o¥-mini¥. Prompt笏ｬﾃ｡engineering is the science笏ｬﾃ｡of structuring input笏ｬﾃ｡instructions to guide a笏ｬﾃ｡language笏ｬﾃ｡model toward笏ｬﾃ｡producing笏ｬﾃ｡specific, accurate, and useful笏ｬﾃ｡outputs¥. Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit's笏ｬﾃ｡prompts include笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe analysis笏ｬﾃ｡data, user笏ｬﾃ｡preference profiles, occasion笏ｬﾃ｡context, weather data, and detailed styling rules¥. The model笏ｬﾃ｡processes笏ｬﾃ｡this笏ｬﾃ｡natural笏ｬﾃ｡language context笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡generates structured笏ｬﾃ｡outfit笏ｬﾃ｡recommendations or笏ｬﾃ｡conversational style笏ｬﾃ｡advice in笏ｬﾃ｡response¥.
- __Algorithmic Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Intelligence笏ｬﾃ｡¥(12笏ｬﾃ｡Custom Algorithms__¥)¥.笏ｬﾃ｡Beyond笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡AI笏ｬﾃ｡model, Fashion Fit implements 12 proprietary scoring笏ｬﾃ｡algorithms笏ｬﾃ｡that evaluate笏ｬﾃ｡outfit quality笏ｬﾃ｡across multiple dimensions: Color笏ｬﾃ｡Harmony Analysis笏ｬﾃ｡¥(based笏ｬﾃ｡on color wheel theory¥), Style Coherence Analysis笏ｬﾃ｡¥(matching笏ｬﾃ｡items笏ｬﾃ｡to style families¥), Occasion Appropriateness笏ｬﾃ｡¥(formality matching¥), Silhouette Balance ¥(fitted笏ｬﾃ｡vs relaxed proportions¥), Pattern Mixing ¥(scale笏ｬﾃ｡contrast笏ｬﾃ｡validation¥), Texture Harmony, Season Appropriateness, Weather笏ｬﾃ｡Adaptation, Outfit笏ｬﾃ｡Completeness, Trend Alignment, Versatility笏ｬﾃ｡Scoring, and Wow Factor¥. Each笏ｬﾃ｡algorithm produces笏ｬﾃ｡a numerical score, and the final笏ｬﾃ｡outfit笏ｬﾃ｡score is a笏ｬﾃ｡weighted average across笏ｬﾃ｡all dimensions¥.
- __Color笏ｬﾃ｡Theory ¥(Color Wheel and Harmony笏ｬﾃ｡Rules¥)¥.__笏ｬﾃ｡The Color Harmony algorithm笏ｬﾃ｡is grounded in classical color theory¥. It implements a digital color wheel mapping complementary, analogous, and monochromatic relationships between colors¥. Neutral笏ｬﾃ｡colors ¥(black, white, gray, navy, beige¥) are treated as笏ｬﾃ｡universal pairings¥. The algorithm scores笏ｬﾃ｡outfit color combinations based on established harmony types笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ for example, a "neutral¥-pop" combination ¥(mostly neutrals with笏ｬﾃ｡one accent color¥) scores 95/100, while clashing non¥-complementary colors score lower¥.

CHAPTER THREE: METHODOLOGY

3¥.1 APPARATUS/MATERIALS REQUIRED

- Laptop/Computer:笏ｬﾃ｡1 unit used笏ｬﾃ｡for writing笏ｬﾃ｡code, running the development笏ｬﾃ｡server, and deploying the application¥.
- Smartphone ¥(Android/iOS¥): 1 unit with a functioning camera used笏ｬﾃ｡for testing笏ｬﾃ｡the application笏ｬﾃ｡during笏ｬﾃ｡development¥.
- Cloudinary: Cloud¥-based image storage笏ｬﾃ｡service used to upload, store, and retrieve笏ｬﾃ｡clothing photographs taken笏ｬﾃ｡by users¥.
- OpenAI API笏ｬﾃ｡¥(GPT¥-4o & GPT¥-4o¥-mini¥): Cloud¥-based artificial笏ｬﾃ｡intelligence service used笏ｬﾃ｡for image¥-based clothing笏ｬﾃ｡recognition, text¥-based categorization, outfit笏ｬﾃ｡generation, and the笏ｬﾃ｡Style笏ｬﾃ｡Coach笏ｬﾃ｡chatbot¥.
- Node¥.js笏ｬﾃ｡& Express¥.js: Backend server framework used to build笏ｬﾃ｡the API that笏ｬﾃ｡handles笏ｬﾃ｡all requests笏ｬﾃ｡between the app and external services¥.
- React Native & Expo ¥(v51¥.0¥): Cross¥-platform mobile application framework used to build the Fashion Fit app for both Android and iOS from a single codebase¥.
- Stable笏ｬﾃ｡Internet笏ｬﾃ｡Connection: Required笏ｬﾃ｡for communicating with the OpenAI API, MongoDB笏ｬﾃ｡database, and Cloudinary image笏ｬﾃ｡storage¥.
- MongoDB笏ｬﾃ｡& Mongoose: NoSQL database used to store user笏ｬﾃ｡accounts, wardrobe items, outfit feedback, user笏ｬﾃ｡preferences, and chat history¥.
- TypeScript: A programming笏ｬﾃ｡language used for writing笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡frontend笏ｬﾃ｡application笏ｬﾃ｡code笏ｬﾃ｡with笏ｬﾃ｡type笏ｬﾃ｡safety and error笏ｬﾃ｡prevention¥.
- React笏ｬﾃ｡Navigation: Library笏ｬﾃ｡used笏ｬﾃ｡to笏ｬﾃ｡build笏ｬﾃ｡the app's screen笏ｬﾃ｡navigation system, including笏ｬﾃ｡tab¥-based and笏ｬﾃ｡stack¥-based navigation¥.

3¥.2 PROCEDURE

1¥. Set笏ｬﾃ｡up the development笏ｬﾃ｡environment by installing Node¥.js, React笏ｬﾃ｡Native, Expo CLI, and TypeScript on笏ｬﾃ｡the development笏ｬﾃ｡computer¥.

2¥. Initialize the笏ｬﾃ｡React Native project using笏ｬﾃ｡Expo and笏ｬﾃ｡configure笏ｬﾃ｡the folder笏ｬﾃ｡structure for screens, services, navigation, and components¥.

3¥. Design and笏ｬﾃ｡build笏ｬﾃ｡the user笏ｬﾃ｡interface screens, including笏ｬﾃ｡Home, Wardrobe, Camera, Stylist, Chat, Planner, Profile, and笏ｬﾃ｡Onboarding¥.

4¥. Set up the backend server笏ｬﾃ｡using Node¥.js and Express¥.js笏ｬﾃ｡with笏ｬﾃ｡API笏ｬﾃ｡routes for authentication, wardrobe management, AI笏ｬﾃ｡categorization, outfit笏ｬﾃ｡styling, and chat¥.

5¥. Connect the backend笏ｬﾃ｡to a笏ｬﾃ｡MongoDB database using Mongoose to笏ｬﾃ｡store user accounts, clothing笏ｬﾃ｡items, outfit feedback, and user preferences¥.

6¥. Integrate the OpenAI GPT¥-4o Vision API to笏ｬﾃ｡receive笏ｬﾃ｡clothing笏ｬﾃ｡photographs笏ｬﾃ｡from笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡smartphone笏ｬﾃ｡camera and return笏ｬﾃ｡automatic笏ｬﾃ｡categorization data笏ｬﾃ｡¥(category, color, style, pattern, fit, occasion, brand, and tags¥)¥.

7¥. Integrate OpenAI GPT¥-4o¥-mini to power笏ｬﾃ｡the AI Stylist for笏ｬﾃ｡generating outfit recommendations笏ｬﾃ｡and the笏ｬﾃ｡Style Coach for笏ｬﾃ｡conversational fashion笏ｬﾃ｡advice¥.

8¥. Built 12 custom fashion intelligence algorithms for笏ｬﾃ｡scoring笏ｬﾃ｡outfits on笏ｬﾃ｡color harmony, style coherence, occasion笏ｬﾃ｡appropriateness, silhouette balance, pattern mixing, texture笏ｬﾃ｡harmony, season笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡weather adaptation, completeness, trend笏ｬﾃ｡alignment, versatility, and wow笏ｬﾃ｡factor¥.

9¥. Integrate Cloudinary for cloud¥-based storage笏ｬﾃ｡of clothing photographs uploaded笏ｬﾃ｡by users¥.

10¥. Built the continuous笏ｬﾃ｡learning system that笏ｬﾃ｡records笏ｬﾃ｡user feedback笏ｬﾃ｡¥(saves, rejections, ratings¥) and updates individual笏ｬﾃ｡preference笏ｬﾃ｡profiles to笏ｬﾃ｡improve future笏ｬﾃ｡recommendations¥.

11¥. Implement navigation笏ｬﾃ｡using笏ｬﾃ｡React Navigation with笏ｬﾃ｡bottom笏ｬﾃ｡tabs and笏ｬﾃ｡stack¥-based screen笏ｬﾃ｡transitions¥.

12¥. Test the application on笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡physical smartphone笏ｬﾃ｡device, fix笏ｬﾃ｡bugs, and verify that笏ｬﾃ｡all features笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ camera笏ｬﾃ｡capture, AI categorization, outfit generation, and chat笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ worked correctly before笏ｬﾃ｡conducting笏ｬﾃ｡experiments¥.

3¥.3 OBSERVATION

During the development and testing of Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit, several笏ｬﾃ｡key observations were made across all four experiments¥. When笏ｬﾃ｡a clothing item was photographed under good笏ｬﾃ｡lighting conditions, the OpenAI GPT¥-4o Vision API consistently returned笏ｬﾃ｡accurate category and color results within笏ｬﾃ｡2 to 3 seconds, making笏ｬﾃ｡the digitization process feel almost instant to笏ｬﾃ｡the user¥. The笏ｬﾃ｡AI performed best on items with distinct silhouettes such as shoes, dresses, and tops, while笏ｬﾃ｡items with overlapping features ﾎ禿僌ｶ such as笏ｬﾃ｡a long oversized top that resembled笏ｬﾃ｡a dress ﾎ禿僌ｶ were occasionally misclassified¥. Color recognition was noticeably笏ｬﾃ｡affected by lighting conditions, with dark items photographed under warm indoor笏ｬﾃ｡lighting sometimes misidentified; for example, navy blue being classified as笏ｬﾃ｡black, or dark green as brown¥.

During manual wardrobe entry, participants frequently hesitated when deciding how to笏ｬﾃ｡categorize items and how to describe colors笏ｬﾃ｡in words, with several asking笏ｬﾃ｡"what category does this fall under?" while writing¥. With Fashion Fit, this hesitation was eliminated entirely since the笏ｬﾃ｡AI made all decisions automatically¥. Similarly, when selecting outfits without the笏ｬﾃ｡app, participants were observed picking up and笏ｬﾃ｡putting down multiple items repeatedly before reaching a笏ｬﾃ｡final decision, with some expressing frustration笏ｬﾃ｡at having "too many options¥." With笏ｬﾃ｡the AI Stylist, most participants笏ｬﾃ｡accepted one of the three suggestions within seconds笏ｬﾃ｡and expressed relief at not having to browse笏ｬﾃ｡through笏ｬﾃ｡their笏ｬﾃ｡entire笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe¥.

On笏ｬﾃ｡the technical笏ｬﾃ｡side, the 12 custom scoring笏ｬﾃ｡algorithms worked effectively in filtering out incomplete or笏ｬﾃ｡poorly matched outfits, with combinations笏ｬﾃ｡missing a top or bottom being automatically rejected笏ｬﾃ｡before being shown to the user¥. Several笏ｬﾃ｡participants in Test 4 were genuinely surprised to discover items in their digitized wardrobe that they had forgotten笏ｬﾃ｡they owned, and笏ｬﾃ｡after the AI Stylist began incorporating笏ｬﾃ｡these items into outfit suggestions, participants started笏ｬﾃ｡using them regularly¥. The continuous learning system笏ｬﾃ｡also笏ｬﾃ｡showed visible improvement after 5 to 10 feedback interactions, with the AI Stylist beginning to favour colors and styles the user had previously saved and avoiding combinations they had rejected¥.

3¥.4 EXPLANATION/DISCUSSION 

The results笏ｬﾃ｡across笏ｬﾃ｡all four tests笏ｬﾃ｡support笏ｬﾃ｡the hypothesis笏ｬﾃ｡that AI¥-powered wardrobe management笏ｬﾃ｡significantly笏ｬﾃ｡outperforms manual methods笏ｬﾃ｡in笏ｬﾃ｡speed, accuracy, and utilization¥. The笏ｬﾃ｡75¥.4% reduction笏ｬﾃ｡in digitization time is explained笏ｬﾃ｡by the elimination笏ｬﾃ｡of manual data笏ｬﾃ｡entry¥. Instead笏ｬﾃ｡of the笏ｬﾃ｡user having笏ｬﾃ｡to identify笏ｬﾃ｡an笏ｬﾃ｡item's category, think笏ｬﾃ｡of how笏ｬﾃ｡to describe its笏ｬﾃ｡color, and write everything笏ｬﾃ｡down by hand, the OpenAI GPT¥-4o Vision笏ｬﾃ｡API processes笏ｬﾃ｡the photograph笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡returns all笏ｬﾃ｡attributes笏ｬﾃ｡automatically¥. The笏ｬﾃ｡bottleneck shifts笏ｬﾃ｡from human cognition and笏ｬﾃ｡writing speed to a 2 to笏ｬﾃ｡3 second API笏ｬﾃ｡call, which is consistently faster regardless of笏ｬﾃ｡the item's complexity¥.

The笏ｬﾃ｡91¥.7% category accuracy is a笏ｬﾃ｡result笏ｬﾃ｡of GPT¥-4o's multimodal笏ｬﾃ｡capabilities¥. Having笏ｬﾃ｡been笏ｬﾃ｡pre¥-trained on billions of image¥-text pairs, the model carries笏ｬﾃ｡broad笏ｬﾃ｡visual understanding of笏ｬﾃ｡clothing types, which笏ｬﾃ｡translates well笏ｬﾃ｡to the six¥-category system used笏ｬﾃ｡by笏ｬﾃ｡Fashion Fit¥. The five笏ｬﾃ｡misclassifications out笏ｬﾃ｡of 60 images occurred笏ｬﾃ｡in笏ｬﾃ｡categories笏ｬﾃ｡with笏ｬﾃ｡visual笏ｬﾃ｡overlap笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ outerwear items笏ｬﾃ｡misidentified as tops, and笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡shoe笏ｬﾃ｡misclassified as a dress笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ which笏ｬﾃ｡is笏ｬﾃ｡consistent笏ｬﾃ｡with the challenge笏ｬﾃ｡of classifying items that笏ｬﾃ｡share similar shapes¥. The lower笏ｬﾃ｡color accuracy of 70¥.0% is explained by the sensitivity笏ｬﾃ｡of color笏ｬﾃ｡detection to lighting conditions¥. Published笏ｬﾃ｡research笏ｬﾃ｡confirms笏ｬﾃ｡that color classification under uncontrolled conditions笏ｬﾃ｡typically falls within the笏ｬﾃ｡70 to 85% range, meaning笏ｬﾃ｡Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit's result笏ｬﾃ｡is笏ｬﾃ｡within expected笏ｬﾃ｡benchmarks rather笏ｬﾃ｡than a笏ｬﾃ｡flaw in笏ｬﾃ｡the system's笏ｬﾃ｡design¥.

The 76¥.2% reduction in outfit decision time is笏ｬﾃ｡explained笏ｬﾃ｡by the choice architecture created笏ｬﾃ｡by the AI Stylist¥. An笏ｬﾃ｡average笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe of笏ｬﾃ｡40 items produces笏ｬﾃ｡thousands笏ｬﾃ｡of possible笏ｬﾃ｡outfit笏ｬﾃ｡combinations, which笏ｬﾃ｡creates笏ｬﾃ｡a condition笏ｬﾃ｡known笏ｬﾃ｡as choice笏ｬﾃ｡overload¥. The AI Stylist eliminates this笏ｬﾃ｡by presenting exactly笏ｬﾃ｡three curated suggestions scored笏ｬﾃ｡by the笏ｬﾃ｡12 custom algorithms笏ｬﾃ｡for笏ｬﾃ｡color笏ｬﾃ｡harmony, style coherence, and occasion笏ｬﾃ｡appropriateness¥. This dramatic笏ｬﾃ｡narrowing of options aligns with decision笏ｬﾃ｡fatigue research, which shows that fewer, higher¥-quality choices lead to faster and笏ｬﾃ｡more confident decisions¥.

The increase笏ｬﾃ｡in wardrobe utilization from笏ｬﾃ｡36¥.3% to 76¥.3% is the most impactful finding from笏ｬﾃ｡a sustainability perspective¥. The AI Stylist is笏ｬﾃ｡designed to draw笏ｬﾃ｡from the full range of a user's笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe when笏ｬﾃ｡generating笏ｬﾃ｡recommendations, including笏ｬﾃ｡items that笏ｬﾃ｡have笏ｬﾃ｡not笏ｬﾃ｡appeared笏ｬﾃ｡in any recent笏ｬﾃ｡outfit¥. This creates笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡"rediscovery effect" where users笏ｬﾃ｡are reminded of clothing笏ｬﾃ｡they forgot they owned and笏ｬﾃ｡begin笏ｬﾃ｡incorporating笏ｬﾃ｡those笏ｬﾃ｡items into their daily笏ｬﾃ｡rotation¥. Participants did笏ｬﾃ｡not buy new clothes during笏ｬﾃ｡the trial笏ｬﾃ｡period笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ they simply began笏ｬﾃ｡using more of what they already had, which is the core sustainability笏ｬﾃ｡goal笏ｬﾃ｡of the笏ｬﾃ｡project¥.

- Graph 1 presents笏ｬﾃ｡a grouped笏ｬﾃ｡bar chart comparing the笏ｬﾃ｡time taken笏ｬﾃ｡by笏ｬﾃ｡four笏ｬﾃ｡participants to digitize笏ｬﾃ｡10 clothing笏ｬﾃ｡items using笏ｬﾃ｡manual笏ｬﾃ｡entry笏ｬﾃ｡versus the笏ｬﾃ｡Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit application¥. The red笏ｬﾃ｡bars represent笏ｬﾃ｡manual entry笏ｬﾃ｡times, which笏ｬﾃ｡ranged from 375¥.7 seconds笏ｬﾃ｡to 451¥.9 seconds across笏ｬﾃ｡all participants¥. The green bars represent Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit times, which ranged from笏ｬﾃ｡68¥.7 seconds to 100¥.7 seconds¥. In笏ｬﾃ｡every笏ｬﾃ｡case, the green笏ｬﾃ｡bar笏ｬﾃ｡is笏ｬﾃ｡dramatically笏ｬﾃ｡shorter than the red bar, visually confirming that Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit is consistently笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡significantly faster than manual笏ｬﾃ｡entry¥. The average笏ｬﾃ｡time reduction笏ｬﾃ｡across笏ｬﾃ｡all participants was 75¥.4%, meaning笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡app笏ｬﾃ｡completed笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡same笏ｬﾃ｡task笏ｬﾃ｡in roughly笏ｬﾃ｡one笏ｬﾃ｡quarter笏ｬﾃ｡of the time required笏ｬﾃ｡by hand¥.

__Graph 2: AI笏ｬﾃ｡Classification笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ Precision, Recall, and笏ｬﾃ｡F1 per笏ｬﾃ｡Category__

- Graph 2 presents a笏ｬﾃ｡grouped笏ｬﾃ｡bar chart showing笏ｬﾃ｡three笏ｬﾃ｡classification笏ｬﾃ｡metrics笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ Precision笏ｬﾃ｡¥(blue¥), Recall ¥(orange¥), and F1 Score ¥(green¥) ﾎ禿僌ｶ for each of the six clothing笏ｬﾃ｡categories¥. All笏ｬﾃ｡bars笏ｬﾃ｡cluster笏ｬﾃ｡between笏ｬﾃ｡0¥.78 and笏ｬﾃ｡1¥.00, indicating strong笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡consistent AI笏ｬﾃ｡performance across every笏ｬﾃ｡category¥. Bottom笏ｬﾃ｡achieved笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡highest F1 score笏ｬﾃ｡¥(0¥.957¥), followed笏ｬﾃ｡by Accessory ¥(0¥.941¥) and Shoes笏ｬﾃ｡¥(0¥.923¥)¥. The weakest category笏ｬﾃ｡was Outerwear, with笏ｬﾃ｡a recall笏ｬﾃ｡of 0¥.786, meaning笏ｬﾃ｡the AI missed笏ｬﾃ｡3笏ｬﾃ｡out of 14 outerwear items by笏ｬﾃ｡misclassifying them as tops笏ｬﾃ｡or笏ｬﾃ｡accessories¥. Despite笏ｬﾃ｡this, the overall笏ｬﾃ｡Macro笏ｬﾃ｡F1 of笏ｬﾃ｡0¥.918 confirms笏ｬﾃ｡that笏ｬﾃ｡the AI delivers笏ｬﾃ｡balanced, reliable笏ｬﾃ｡classification笏ｬﾃ｡performance and笏ｬﾃ｡is笏ｬﾃ｡not simply笏ｬﾃ｡achieving笏ｬﾃ｡high笏ｬﾃ｡accuracy by笏ｬﾃ｡excelling in only笏ｬﾃ｡one or笏ｬﾃ｡two categories¥.

__Graph 3: Outfit Decision Time ﾎ禿僌ｶ Without App笏ｬﾃ｡vs AI笏ｬﾃ｡Stylist__

- Graph 3 presents a grouped bar chart comparing the笏ｬﾃ｡time taken by four participants to choose笏ｬﾃ｡an outfit without笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡app笏ｬﾃ｡versus using笏ｬﾃ｡the AI笏ｬﾃ｡Stylist¥. The red bars represent decision笏ｬﾃ｡times笏ｬﾃ｡without笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡app, ranging from 254¥.1 seconds to 433¥.4 seconds¥. The green bars represent decision笏ｬﾃ｡times with the AI笏ｬﾃ｡Stylist, ranging from just笏ｬﾃ｡36¥.8 seconds to 86¥.9 seconds¥. The contrast笏ｬﾃ｡between笏ｬﾃ｡the two is笏ｬﾃ｡striking笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ in笏ｬﾃ｡the most笏ｬﾃ｡extreme笏ｬﾃ｡case, Participant 1 took笏ｬﾃ｡433笏ｬﾃ｡seconds without the app but笏ｬﾃ｡only 37 seconds with the AI Stylist, a 91¥.5% reduction¥. The笏ｬﾃ｡average reduction笏ｬﾃ｡across all participants was 76¥.2%, demonstrating that the AI Stylist eliminates the笏ｬﾃ｡majority笏ｬﾃ｡of time笏ｬﾃ｡spent browsing and deliberating by笏ｬﾃ｡presenting笏ｬﾃ｡three笏ｬﾃ｡ready¥-made笏ｬﾃ｡outfit笏ｬﾃ｡options¥.

__Graph 4: Wardrobe Utilization ﾎ禿僌ｶ Before vs笏ｬﾃ｡After Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit__

- Graph 4笏ｬﾃ｡presents a grouped bar chart comparing the wardrobe utilization rate笏ｬﾃ｡of笏ｬﾃ｡four笏ｬﾃ｡participants before and after using Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit¥. The red bars represent utilization before the笏ｬﾃ｡app, ranging from 31¥.7% to 48¥.3%, meaning笏ｬﾃ｡participants笏ｬﾃ｡were笏ｬﾃ｡actively笏ｬﾃ｡wearing笏ｬﾃ｡less笏ｬﾃ｡than half笏ｬﾃ｡of their clothing¥. The green bars represent utilization after using笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡app, ranging from 69¥.0% to 86¥.0%¥. Every笏ｬﾃ｡participant showed笏ｬﾃ｡a substantial笏ｬﾃ｡increase, with the largest笏ｬﾃ｡jump being笏ｬﾃ｡Participant笏ｬﾃ｡3, who笏ｬﾃ｡went笏ｬﾃ｡from 31¥.7% to笏ｬﾃ｡85¥.4%笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ an笏ｬﾃ｡increase笏ｬﾃ｡of 53¥.7 percentage points¥. The average increase across all participants was笏ｬﾃ｡40¥.0笏ｬﾃ｡percentage points, meaning笏ｬﾃ｡users笏ｬﾃ｡more笏ｬﾃ｡than doubled the proportion of their wardrobe that was笏ｬﾃ｡actively used¥. This is笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡strongest笏ｬﾃ｡evidence of笏ｬﾃ｡Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit's sustainability笏ｬﾃ｡impact, showing笏ｬﾃ｡that the app helps笏ｬﾃ｡users wear笏ｬﾃ｡more of what笏ｬﾃ｡they already笏ｬﾃ｡own rather than buying笏ｬﾃ｡new clothing¥.

4¥.3 DATA INTERPRETATION

The笏ｬﾃ｡paired笏ｬﾃ｡t¥-test results for笏ｬﾃ｡Test笏ｬﾃ｡1 ¥(Time¥-to¥-Digitize¥) produced笏ｬﾃ｡a t¥-statistic of 36¥.180, which is far笏ｬﾃ｡greater than the critical value of笏ｬﾃ｡2¥.201 at the 95% confidence level¥. This means笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡75¥.4% time reduction is笏ｬﾃ｡statistically significant and笏ｬﾃ｡not due to chance¥. We笏ｬﾃ｡are笏ｬﾃ｡95% confident that the true time笏ｬﾃ｡saved when笏ｬﾃ｡digitizing 10 clothing笏ｬﾃ｡items lies between笏ｬﾃ｡5¥.0 and 5¥.6 minutes¥. In笏ｬﾃ｡simple笏ｬﾃ｡terms, what笏ｬﾃ｡takes笏ｬﾃ｡7 minutes by笏ｬﾃ｡hand takes less笏ｬﾃ｡than笏ｬﾃ｡2 minutes with Fashion Fit, and笏ｬﾃ｡this笏ｬﾃ｡difference is scientifically proven笏ｬﾃ｡to be real¥. Similarly, the paired笏ｬﾃ｡t¥-test for Test笏ｬﾃ｡3笏ｬﾃ｡¥(Outfit Decision Time¥) produced a t¥-statistic of 12¥.813笏ｬﾃ｡against笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡critical value of 2¥.145, confirming that the笏ｬﾃ｡76¥.2% reduction笏ｬﾃ｡in decision time is also笏ｬﾃ｡statistically significant¥. Users笏ｬﾃ｡save笏ｬﾃ｡between笏ｬﾃ｡3¥.4 and笏ｬﾃ｡4¥.7 minutes every笏ｬﾃ｡time they choose笏ｬﾃ｡an笏ｬﾃ｡outfit using笏ｬﾃ｡the笏ｬﾃ｡AI笏ｬﾃ｡Stylist instead笏ｬﾃ｡of browsing their笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe manually¥.

For笏ｬﾃ｡Test笏ｬﾃ｡2 ¥(AI Categorization Accuracy¥), the AI correctly笏ｬﾃ｡identified笏ｬﾃ｡the clothing笏ｬﾃ｡category笏ｬﾃ｡in笏ｬﾃ｡55笏ｬﾃ｡out of 60 images, achieving笏ｬﾃ｡91¥.7% accuracy and笏ｬﾃ｡exceeding the 85% target笏ｬﾃ｡set笏ｬﾃ｡in the hypothesis¥. The Macro F1 score of 0¥.918 confirms that this accuracy is consistent笏ｬﾃ｡across all six categories笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ the笏ｬﾃ｡AI performs笏ｬﾃ｡well on笏ｬﾃ｡tops, bottoms, dresses, shoes, outerwear, and accessories, not笏ｬﾃ｡just one笏ｬﾃ｡or two¥. The five笏ｬﾃ｡errors笏ｬﾃ｡occurred笏ｬﾃ｡in categories笏ｬﾃ｡with笏ｬﾃ｡visual笏ｬﾃ｡overlap, such as outerwear being笏ｬﾃ｡confused笏ｬﾃ｡with tops¥. Color笏ｬﾃ｡accuracy was 70¥.0%, which笏ｬﾃ｡is lower but笏ｬﾃ｡consistent笏ｬﾃ｡with published笏ｬﾃ｡research showing that uncontrolled lighting makes笏ｬﾃ｡color笏ｬﾃ｡detection笏ｬﾃ｡difficult¥. The two笏ｬﾃ｡human笏ｬﾃ｡labelers agreed笏ｬﾃ｡on笏ｬﾃ｡90¥.0% of images笏ｬﾃ｡with笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡Cohen's Kappa of笏ｬﾃ｡0¥.879, classified笏ｬﾃ｡as "almost perfect agreement," confirming笏ｬﾃ｡that the benchmark used笏ｬﾃ｡to笏ｬﾃ｡judge笏ｬﾃ｡the AI was笏ｬﾃ｡fair and reliable¥.

For笏ｬﾃ｡Test笏ｬﾃ｡2 ¥(AI Categorization Accuracy¥), the AI correctly笏ｬﾃ｡identified笏ｬﾃ｡the clothing笏ｬﾃ｡category笏ｬﾃ｡in笏ｬﾃ｡55笏ｬﾃ｡out of 60 images, achieving笏ｬﾃ｡91¥.7% accuracy and笏ｬﾃ｡exceeding the 85% target笏ｬﾃ｡set笏ｬﾃ｡in the hypothesis¥. The Macro F1 score of 0¥.918 confirms that this accuracy is consistent笏ｬﾃ｡across all six categories笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ the笏ｬﾃ｡AI performs笏ｬﾃ｡well on笏ｬﾃ｡tops, bottoms, dresses, shoes, outerwear, and accessories, not笏ｬﾃ｡just one笏ｬﾃ｡or two¥. The five笏ｬﾃ｡errors笏ｬﾃ｡occurred笏ｬﾃ｡in categories笏ｬﾃ｡with笏ｬﾃ｡visual笏ｬﾃ｡overlap, such as outerwear being笏ｬﾃ｡confused笏ｬﾃ｡with tops¥. Color笏ｬﾃ｡accuracy was 70¥.0%, which笏ｬﾃ｡is lower but笏ｬﾃ｡consistent笏ｬﾃ｡with published笏ｬﾃ｡research showing that uncontrolled lighting makes笏ｬﾃ｡color笏ｬﾃ｡detection笏ｬﾃ｡difficult¥. The two笏ｬﾃ｡human笏ｬﾃ｡labelers agreed笏ｬﾃ｡on笏ｬﾃ｡90¥.0% of images笏ｬﾃ｡with笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡Cohen's Kappa of笏ｬﾃ｡0¥.879, classified笏ｬﾃ｡as "almost perfect agreement," confirming笏ｬﾃ｡that the benchmark used笏ｬﾃ｡to笏ｬﾃ｡judge笏ｬﾃ｡the AI was笏ｬﾃ｡fair and reliable¥.

CHAPTER FIVE: CONCLUSION AND RECOMMENDATION

5¥.1 CONCLUSION

This笏ｬﾃ｡project笏ｬﾃ｡set笏ｬﾃ｡out to investigate笏ｬﾃ｡whether an笏ｬﾃ｡AI¥-powered mobile笏ｬﾃ｡application笏ｬﾃ｡could meaningfully improve笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe management, reduce daily笏ｬﾃ｡outfit笏ｬﾃ｡decision time, and promote笏ｬﾃ｡sustainable clothing笏ｬﾃ｡use¥. Four笏ｬﾃ｡controlled笏ｬﾃ｡experiments笏ｬﾃ｡were designed笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡conducted, each笏ｬﾃ｡targeting笏ｬﾃ｡a specific笏ｬﾃ｡aspect笏ｬﾃ｡of the Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit application, and笏ｬﾃ｡all笏ｬﾃ｡results笏ｬﾃ｡were evaluated笏ｬﾃ｡using paired笏ｬﾃ｡t¥-tests at the 95% confidence level¥. The findings confirm笏ｬﾃ｡that every笏ｬﾃ｡part笏ｬﾃ｡of the hypothesis笏ｬﾃ｡was met笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡exceeded¥.

Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit reduced笏ｬﾃ｡wardrobe digitization time by 75¥.4%, cutting笏ｬﾃ｡what笏ｬﾃ｡previously笏ｬﾃ｡took 7 minutes down笏ｬﾃ｡to under笏ｬﾃ｡2 minutes for笏ｬﾃ｡10 clothing笏ｬﾃ｡items¥. The笏ｬﾃ｡AI classification笏ｬﾃ｡system笏ｬﾃ｡achieved 91¥.7% category笏ｬﾃ｡accuracy with笏ｬﾃ｡a笏ｬﾃ｡Macro F1 score of笏ｬﾃ｡0¥.918, surpassing the 85% target笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡proving笏ｬﾃ｡that automated笏ｬﾃ｡labeling is reliable笏ｬﾃ｡enough笏ｬﾃ｡to replace笏ｬﾃ｡manual data笏ｬﾃ｡entry¥. The AI笏ｬﾃ｡Stylist reduced笏ｬﾃ｡outfit decision time by 76¥.2%, saving笏ｬﾃ｡users笏ｬﾃ｡an笏ｬﾃ｡average of笏ｬﾃ｡4笏ｬﾃ｡minutes per decision笏ｬﾃ｡by笏ｬﾃ｡presenting笏ｬﾃ｡three curated outfit suggestions笏ｬﾃ｡instead of requiring笏ｬﾃ｡them to browse an笏ｬﾃ｡entire wardrobe¥. Most笏ｬﾃ｡importantly, wardrobe utilization increased from笏ｬﾃ｡36¥.3% to 76¥.3%笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ a 40笏ｬﾃ｡percentage point jump笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ meaning笏ｬﾃ｡participants笏ｬﾃ｡more笏ｬﾃ｡than doubled the proportion of clothing笏ｬﾃ｡they actively wore, all笏ｬﾃ｡without笏ｬﾃ｡purchasing a笏ｬﾃ｡single new item¥.

These笏ｬﾃ｡results demonstrate that artificial笏ｬﾃ｡intelligence, when applied笏ｬﾃ｡thoughtfully to笏ｬﾃ｡an笏ｬﾃ｡everyday笏ｬﾃ｡problem, can deliver笏ｬﾃ｡real, measurable benefits in笏ｬﾃ｡efficiency, accuracy, and environmental笏ｬﾃ｡sustainability¥. Fashion笏ｬﾃ｡Fit is笏ｬﾃ｡not just a convenience笏ｬﾃ｡tool笏ｬﾃ｡ﾎ禿僌ｶ it is a practical笏ｬﾃ｡solution to the笏ｬﾃ｡global笏ｬﾃ｡challenge of clothing笏ｬﾃ｡waste, starting笏ｬﾃ｡in笏ｬﾃ｡the individual's笏ｬﾃ｡closet¥. The project bridges笏ｬﾃ｡computer笏ｬﾃ｡science, cognitive笏ｬﾃ｡psychology, and sustainability science笏ｬﾃ｡into笏ｬﾃ｡a single笏ｬﾃ｡mobile笏ｬﾃ｡application that is笏ｬﾃ｡accessible笏ｬﾃ｡to笏ｬﾃ｡any笏ｬﾃ｡smartphone笏ｬﾃ｡user in笏ｬﾃ｡Kenya笏ｬﾃ｡and笏ｬﾃ｡beyond¥. All笏ｬﾃ｡four笏ｬﾃ｡tests笏ｬﾃ｡produced笏ｬﾃ｡statistically significant results, providing笏ｬﾃ｡strong笏ｬﾃ｡scientific笏ｬﾃ｡evidence that笏ｬﾃ｡the approach笏ｬﾃ｡works and is ready笏ｬﾃ｡for wider笏ｬﾃ｡adoption¥.

__5__¥.4 POSSIBLE EXPANSION

- __"Worn Today" tracking feature: __Implement a daily log where users confirm which outfit they actually wore¥. This would allow wardrobe utilization to be measured from real daily wear data rather than saved or planned outfits, providing even stronger sustainability evidence¥.
- __Improved color accuracy: __Train the AI with a larger, more diverse image dataset captured under varied lighting conditions and introduce automatic white¥-balance correction before color classification to improve the current 70¥.0% accuracy¥.
- __Expanded clothing categories: __Add subcategories such as formal top, casual top, and athletic top, as well as support for traditional and cultural garments like kitenge, kikoi, and shuka to better serve the Kenyan and East African market¥.
- __Offline AI processing: __Develop on¥-device AI models that can categorize clothing without an internet connection, addressing connectivity challenges in rural areas and reducing dependency on cloud¥-based processing¥.
- __Social and sharing features: __Allow users to share outfit suggestions with friends for feedback, or browse anonymized outfit ideas from other users with similar wardrobes, creating a community¥-driven styling experience¥.
